author: f1

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

In 2018, the Marina Bay circuit was considered a
„weak“ track for Mercedes, with Ferrari and Red Bull expected to dominate. After struggling through practice and barely making it out of Q1, Lewis Hamilton produced a lap in Q3 that defied all team Simulations and logic. Clocking a 1:36.015, Hamilton was over six-tenths faster than his title rival Sebastian Vettel. The lap was a masterclass in precision, as he danced the car inches away from the walls with zero corrections, finding grip where none seemed to exist. Team Principal Toto Wolff famously called it „stardust,“ noting that Hamilton had actually beaten the car’s theoretical limit. It remains one of the most iconic moments in F1 history, effectively breaking the spirit of his rivals and securing a legendary pole position.

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

Abu Dhabi. 2010.
Sebastian Vettel crossed the finish line and won the race.
But the championship wasn’t decided yet.
Fernando Alonso and Mark Webber were still on track.
For a few seconds, the entire Red Bull garage waited.
Mechanics watching the timing screens.
Engineers calculating the points.
If Alonso finished high enough, the title was gone.
Then the final positions became clear.
Alonso - seventh.
And the radio finally came alive.
„Thank you boys. Thank you. Unbelievable.“
Then the words that changed everything:
„Sebastian Vettel, you are the world champion! The world champion!“
At 23 years old, Vettel became the youngest Formula 1 world champion in history.
A title decided not just at the finish line -
but in the final seconds of the season.

author: f1wow

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

From a smiling underdog to one of the most loved drivers in Formula 1, Daniel Ricciardo made his debut in 2011 and quickly became known for his late-braking „divebomb“ overtakes.

He rose to fame with Red Bull Racing, taking multiple wins and even beating champions on pure skill. His bold move to Renault and later McLaren showed his fearless mindset highlighted by his iconic Monza 2021 win, ending McLaren’s long victory drought Known for his charisma, „shoey“ celebrations and aggressive racing style, Ricciardo isn’t just fast - he’s a fan favorite who brings personality to the grid.
author: 5secondpenalty

author: motorsportcom

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

One of the most famous drives of Lewis Hamilton’s career came at the 2021 So Paulo Grand Prix. After being disqualified from qualifying and taking engine penalties, Hamilton started the sprint race from P20 and stormed through the field to finish P5. He then started the Grand Prix from P10 and produced another remarkable comeback, overtaking rivals one by one before passing Max Verstappen to take victory. Across the sprint and the race, he effectively went from the back of the grid to winning the weekend, a performance widely regarded as one of the greatest comeback drives in modern Formula 1 history.
author: 5secondpenalty

author: motorsportcom

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

Formula 1 had always been a proving ground where rookies were expected to learn, adapt, and slowly climb the ladder. But in 2007, Lewis Hamilton tore that script apart. Entering the sport with McLaren, there was no gentle introduction, no easing into the chaos. From the very first race, Hamilton was thrown into the fire - battling world champions, seasoned veterans, and the relentless spotlight of the biggest stage in motorsport.

What followed was nothing short of astonishing.
Podium after podium, race after race, Hamilton displayed a consistency that defied logic for a newcomer. He raced without fear, without hesitation, armed only with raw pace and unshakable belief. The paddock quickly realized this wasn’t a typical rookie story - this was something extraordinary. The shock deepened when Hamilton wasn’t just competing; he was fighting for the championship itself.

Against his own teammate, reigning champion Fernando Alonso, Hamilton engaged in one of the fiercest intra-team duels Formula 1 had ever witnessed. No hierarchy, no deference, no backing down - just pure, uncompromising competition.
author: 5secondpenalty

author: motorsportcom

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

Niki Lauda - 3x Formula 1 World Champion (1975, 1977, 1984).

Started his F1 career in 1971 and became one of the most fearless drivers in motorsport history. He won championships with both Scuderia Ferrari and McLaren- something very few drivers ever achieved.

In 1976, Lauda suffered a horrific crash at the Nürburgring. His Ferrari caught fire, leaving him with severe burns and lung damage. Doctors thought he wouldn’t survive... but just 42 days later, he returned to race at Monza and finished P4. One of the greatest comebacks in sports history.

After retiring once, he came back again with McLaren and won his third world title in 1984 by just half a point against Alain Prost.
author: 5secondpenalty

author: dailyf1center

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

Carlos Sainz and Charles Leclerc had one of the healthiest teammate relationships in modern Formula 1. They raced together at Ferrari from 2021 to 2024, where they were incredibly closely matched. Both wanted to beat each other every weekend, but neither let that competition damage the respect they had for one another.

Off the track, they became genuine friends. Fans loved seeing them together because their chemistry felt natural... they were constantly joking in Ferrari videos, teasing each other, and sharing plenty of laughs. One of the best examples came after the 2024 Azerbaijan Grand Prix, when travel disruptions meant they rented a van and drove back to Monaco together, with Charles joking that Carlos was a
„10/10 chauffeur.“

Even after Carlos left Ferrari, they continued to speak highly of each other. Charles has said Carlos made him a better driver, while Carlos has described Charles as one of the toughest teammates he has ever had. Their relationship is a great example of two elite competitors pushing each other to the limit while still maintaining a genuine friendship and mutual respect.

author: dailyf1center

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

Nobody owned Monaco the way Senna did.
Not even close. His relationship with the Monte Carlo street circuit goes beyond six victories, eight podiums, and five pole positions in just ten Grand Prix starts.

It started in 1984. His fifth ever F1 race. In a Toleman
- one of the slowest cars on the grid. Pouring rain.
He was closing on Prost at over two seconds a lap when the race was controversially red flagged. He was robbed. But Monaco had seen enough to know.

1988. The greatest qualifying lap in F1 history
- and not a single TV camera caught it. He out-qualified his McLaren teammate by 1.427 seconds. Then on race day - 50 seconds ahead of Prost and cruising. Then a moment of distraction. He hit the wall at Portier. Walked back to his apartment two blocks away. Sat alone in silence.
That crash haunted him. And fuelled everything that came after. There was something supernatural about Ayrton in Monaco. He described his 1988 qualifying lap himself: „Suddenly I realised I was no longer driving the car consciously. I was in a different dimension.“

1992. Williams had the fastest car by miles.
Mansell built a 30 second lead. Pitted for tyres. Came out behind Senna with fresher rubber and a much quicker car. Senna put on a defensive masterclass — beating Mansell by just 0.215 seconds after holding him off for eight laps.

1993. Starting third behind Prost and Schumacher. Crashed in practice and injured his hand. Still won - for the sixth time. Breaking Graham Hill’s record. Damon Hill finished second and said his father would have been the first to congratulate Senna.

Six wins. Five poles. Eight podiums. Ten starts.
He didn’t race Monaco. He communed with it.
author: 5secondpenalty

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

The whole „Smooth Operator“ penalty situation with Carlos Sainz is one of F1’s greatest running jokes, and the best part is that it was never even real. 

It all started in 2019 when Carlos sang Sade’s hit after a good result without even knowing what it meant, and his engineers had to explain it to him. The joke took off, and when he later sang it at Ferrari, his engineer jokingly told him the FIA was banning him due to copyright, to which Carlos responded with a dramatic „this is unacceptable.“ The team kept the joke going by threatening a 5-second penalty if he kept singing it, because apparently being a smooth operator is a criminal offence in the Ferrari garage. Carlos later confirmed he can sing it whenever he wants, and even debunked the myth himself, laughing and saying „That’s not true, that’s clickbait that you believed.“ 

The audacity of making up a fake penalty to mess with your own driver is peak F1 humour, and Carlos playing along is exactly why the Smooth Operator legend will never die.
author: 5secondpenalty

caption:
Guess who’s back

————-
At the 2026 Austrian Grand Prix, Scuderia Ferrari HP brought a highly anticipated, upgraded power unit capitalized through the FIA’s performance catch-up mechanism (ADUO). Following Lewis Hamilton’s breakthrough victory for the team in Barcelona two weeks prior, the Italian squad showed strong pace at the short, high-altitude Red Bull Ring. Drivers Charles Leclerc and Hamilton initially locked down impressive second and third place spots on the starting grid during Saturday’s qualifying. However, Sunday’s 71-lap race proved difficult for the Scuderia. Hamilton’s race execution was hampered by an unsuccessful three-stop strategic plan, resulting in a fifth-place finish, while Leclerc crossed the finish line a lap down in eighth place.

#formula1 #f1memes #scuderiaferrari #lewishamilton #charlesleclerc

author: f1wow

caption:
Races where Lewis Hamilton has avoided the Ferrari simulator in 2026:

🇨🇳 China: P3
🇨🇦 Canada: P2
🇲🇨 Monaco: P2
🇪🇸 Spain: P1 🏆

————-
Following his decision to bypass Scuderia Ferrari’s race-preparation simulator due to data correlation issues, Lewis Hamilton experienced a sensational resurgence across the 2026 season, delivering masterful performances in China, Canada, Monaco, and Spain. The streak began at the Chinese Grand Prix, where he skipped the simulator to secure an impressive P3 finish, marking his first-ever podium in Ferrari red. Although he briefly returned to the virtual rig for a frustrating weekend in Miami, Hamilton completely abandoned it afterward, immediately reaping the rewards at the Canadian Grand Prix with a brilliant P2 podium finish. He carried this old-school momentum directly to the streets of Monte Carlo for the Monaco Grand Prix, skipping the simulator once more to secure another stellar P2 finish and equal Ayrton Senna’s historic record of eight career podiums in the Principality. This incredible run culminated at the Spanish Grand Prix in Barcelona, where a perfectly dialed-in car allowed Hamilton to capture a commanding P1 victory, sealing his emotional maiden race win for Scuderia Ferrari.

#formula1 #f1memes #lewishamilton #scuderiaferrari #charlesleclerc

caption:
It’s getting tight at the top 

———-
The Formula 1 Drivers’ Championship has suddenly tightened into a thrilling battle between championship leader Kimi Antonelli and seven-time world champion Lewis Hamilton. Following a dramatic race weekend at the 2026 Barcelona Grand Prix, Antonelli’s massive 66-point lead was slashed down to just 41 points. Hamilton capitalized on a late mechanical DNF from the young Italian rookie to storm to his magnificent maiden victory for Ferrari, collecting a maximum 25 points. With Antonelli still holding the top spot at 156 points and Hamilton surging in second with 115 points, this unexpected swing has injected fresh momentum into the title race, proving that the battle for the 2026 crown is far from over.
#formula1 #f1memes #lewishamilton #kimiantonelli #racing

caption:
Like a shark smelling blood in the water

———
Lewis Hamilton secured a historic first victory for Scuderia Ferrari at the 2026 Barcelona-Catalunya Grand Prix, marking a monumental milestone in his legendary career. Executing a brilliant three-stop strategy, Hamilton capitalized on a timely Virtual Safety Car on lap 40 to maintain his lead and break his long winless drought in spectacular fashion. This emotional triumph marks his 106th career Formula 1 victory and breaks Michael Schumacher’s record to make Hamilton the most successful driver in Barcelona’s history with seven total wins. Leading an all-British podium ahead of George Russell and Lando Norris, Hamilton capitalized on a late-race engine failure by championship leader Kimi Antonelli to narrow the title fight to 41 points.

#formula1 #f1memes #lewishamilton #scuderiaferrari #f1
author: f1troll

caption:
Credits where credits due

———
Scuderia Ferrari executed a flawless strategic masterclass at the 2026 Barcelona Grand Prix, allowing Lewis Hamilton to claim his highly anticipated, historic first victory for the Italian outfit. Recognizing intense thermal degradation on the high-temperature circuit, Ferrari chose a hyper-aggressive three-stop strategy to counter the traditional two-stop plan favored by Mercedes. The pivotal moment unfolded on lap 40 when a track stoppage triggered a Virtual Safety Car. Pouncing instantly, Ferrari pulled Hamilton into the pitlane for a heavily discounted “free” pit stop. Armed with fresher rubber, Hamilton unleashed a scorching pace that totally neutralised George Russell, eventually pulling away to cross the line with an undisputed 19.5-second lead.

#formula1 #f1memes #lewishamilton #scuderiaferrari #f1

author: f1troll

caption:
Some GOAT problems 🐐 

————
Lewis Hamilton secured a historic and emotional victory at the 2026 Spanish Grand Prix, marking his very first race win for Scuderia Ferrari. After starting on the front row in P2 alongside pole-sitter George Russell, Hamilton capitalized on an aggressive three-stop strategy and a perfectly timed mid-race Virtual Safety Car to leapfrog his rivals. The 41-year-old seven-time world champion put on a masterclass in tyre management in the scorching Barcelona heat, crossing the finish line ahead of Russell and Lando Norris. This landmark triumph broke a 686-day win drought for Hamilton, secured his 106th career victory, and made him the first British driver to win a main Grand Prix for Ferrari since Eddie Irvine in 1999.

#formula1 #f1memes #lewishamilton #scuderiaferrari #f1

caption:
The Kardashian curse and Ferrari curse canceled each other out

——————
The pairing of Formula 1 icon Lewis Hamilton and reality star Kim Kardashian has sparked a wave of viral theories that two of pop culture’s most infamous hexes—the “Kardashian curse” and the “Ferrari curse”—are actively canceling each other out. For years, fans jokingly blamed the Kardashian curse for the career slumps of athletes who dated into the family, while motorsport enthusiasts equally dreaded the “Ferrari curse” of strategic blunders and inconsistent machinery that plagued the Scuderia. However, following their public relationship launch, Hamilton shattered expectations during the Monaco Grand Prix weekend. With Kardashian cheering from the Ferrari garage, Hamilton powered through to a stellar second-place finish. The internet quickly dubbed this phenomenon a real-world “Reverse Curse Technique”, jokingly proving that multiplying two negatives can sometimes create a podium-finishing positive.

#formula1 #f1memes #lewishamilton #kimkardashian #f1

caption:
Absolute depression 

————
During the Q3 session of qualifying at the 2026 Barcelona-Catalunya Grand Prix, Ferrari’s Charles Leclerc suffered a heavy, head-on crash after losing control of his car at Turn 4, sending him directly across the gravel into the tire barriers. Though he walked away uninjured, the incident severely damaged his upgraded SF-26 and relegated him to a 10th-place starting position on the grid. In a deeply emotional post-race interview with Sky Sports, a dejected Leclerc refused to blame his newly swapped brake discs, stating he felt “very much ashamed” and disappointed for making a mistake on a weekend where the car possessed pole-winning pace.
#formula1 #f1memes #charlesleclerc #spanishgp #f1

caption:
Our season starts this week! 
Join us on Patreon on Tuesday, 3rd March, for a close look at all the details in our 2026 liveries. 

And on Youtube on Wednesday, 4th March for our season opener episode. (It’s the longest video we’ve ever made(/making, still working on it).

This marks the first major evolutionary upgrade for our characters since 2020. Excited for a new season! 

#f1 #formula1 #australiangp
